questions.question: What happens when code inside a 'try' block raises an exception?
questions.explanation: When an exception occurs in the try block, execution immediately moves to the matching except block.

questions.question: Which exception is raised when you try to divide by zero?
questions.explanation: ZeroDivisionError is raised when you attempt to divide a number by zero.

questions.question: When does the 'else' block in a try/except/else structure run?
options: Regardless of whether an exception occurs
questions.explanation: The else block runs only if no exception was raised in the try block.

questions.question: When does the 'finally' block run?
options: Only if there is no else block
questions.explanation: The finally block always executes, whether an exception occurred or not. It is typically used for cleanup.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: try: ... except ValueError __ e: print(e)
What keyword do you use to manually trigger an exception inside your code?
